nodes.name: When Executed by WF-001
nodes.notes: Input data mode = Accept all data (passThrough). n8n source: when inputSource==PASSTHROUGH the trigger returns inputData unchanged, INCLUDING binary. Required so binary from the parent Execute Workflow node reaches this sub-workflow. typeVersion 1.1 (1 is deprecated).

nodes.name: Load PR-002 + LLM Settings

parameters.query: SELECT (SELECT settings FROM configuration.providers WHERE kind='llm') AS settings, system_prompt, user_prompt FROM configuration.prompt_versions WHERE prompt_id='PR-002' ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1;

nodes.notes: Loads prompt body (R-08/§608, never embedded) + llm settings. CRIE Postgres credential.

nodes.name: Build LLM Request
nodes.notes: Metadata is document-level, so SW-007 does NOT batch. It sends a BOUNDED front-matter window (first metadataParagraphs paragraphs + title/sectionHeading roles within metadataScanPages) in ONE call. Batching metadata would yield duplicate/conflicting values and waste tokens. Window size is authored config (R-08). PR-002 is unchanged. Typically <5k tokens.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Completions

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: $json._model, temperature: $json._temp, max_tokens: $json._max, response_format: { type: 'json_object' }, messages: $json.messages }) }}

nodes.notes: Credentialed OpenAI call. Swap provider by editing this node only (§360).

meta.note: LLM via HTTP Request node; prompt loaded from configuration.prompt_versions (never embedded).
meta.enrichment: output = {...incomingItem, newResult}; never discards accumulated state
meta.jsonbFix: settings is jsonb (object); removed JSON.parse on settings; LLM-content parse retained
meta.triggerFix: executeWorkflowTrigger upgraded to 1.1 inputSource=passThrough for binary passthrough
changelog: v1.5.0: Build LLM Request no longer serializes the whole ocr object (which embeds ocr.raw = the full Azure analyzeResult). It now substitutes flattened page/paragraph-marked plain text, cutting the prompt ~25x and keeping it inside the model context. Page/paragraph markers preserved for PR-001 sourcePage/sourceParagraph. Adds maxPromptChars budget.

v1.6.0: flattenOcr prefers provider paragraphs (Azure analyzeResult.paragraphs via ocr.pages[].paragraphs), falling back to blank-line splitting only when absent. Emits document statistics {ocrPages, ocrCharacters, approxPromptTokens, truncated, paragraphSource} to the execution log and onto _docStats.

v2.0.0: consumes the normalized OCR model. flattenOcr walks ocr.paragraphs[] (universal unit) instead of ocr.pages[].text (which collapsed DOCX to one page and discarded the document). Locators are [pN:paraM] for paginated formats and [paraM] for DOCX. Stats now include ocrParagraphs, sourceFormat and pagination.

v3.0.0: metadata now extracted from a BOUNDED FRONT-MATTER WINDOW in a single call, instead of flattening the entire document (which both wasted tokens and invited spurious metadata from body text). Window = first metadataParagraphs paragraphs + early title/sectionHeading roles. PR-002 unchanged. Deliberately NOT batched: metadata is document-level, so batching would produce duplicate and conflicting values.
